name: La Serra Gelada – Circular from Benidorm via Altea Port and Alfás del Pi
name_original: La Serra Gelada – Circular desde Benidorm por Puerto de Altea y Alfás del Pi
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Benidorm feature a diverse landscape, blending coastal paths with challenging mountain terrain. The region is characterized by the rugged Sierra Helada Natural Park, the higher peaks of Sierra de Aitana and Puig Campana, and smooth coastal promenades. This variety offers routes ranging from gentle seaside rides to demanding climbs through pine forests and along cliff edges. Best touring cycling routes around Benidorm The most popular touring cycling route is Benidorm: The Cross and Tossal de la Cala Trail, a 18.0 miles (28.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 14 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes significant elevation gain and panoramic views. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is La Serra Gelada – Circular from Benidorm via Altea Port and Alfás del Pi, a difficult 23.3 miles (37.4 km) path. This route traverses the Sierra Helada Natural Park, offering coastal scenery and mountain challenges. Local touring cyclists also love the Balcón del Mediterráneo – Poniente Beach Promenade loop from Levante Beach, a 22.1 miles (35.5 km) trail leading through coastal promenades and urban sections, often completed in about 2 hours 13 minutes. Touring cycling around Benidorm is defined by coastal paths, the rugged terrain of Sierra Helada, and the challenging climbs of the Sierra de Aitana. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy coastal rides to difficult mountain ascents. The routes in Benidorm are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 280 reviews. More than 5,400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Benidorm's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Benidorm?
A: There are over 80 touring cycling routes around Benidorm, catering to various skill levels. You'll find 14 easy routes, 30 moderate routes, and 39 challenging routes, ensuring options for every cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Benidorm's touring cycling routes?
A: Benidorm offers a diverse cycling landscape. You can enjoy smooth coastal paths along promenades like the Paseo de Poniente, or tackle more rugged terrain and challenging climbs through areas like the Sierra Helada Natural Park and the Sierra de Aitana. Routes often combine stunning sea views with mountain vistas.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Benidorm?
A: Yes, many routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952587071/la-serra-gelada-circular-from-benidorm-via-altea-port-and-alfas-del-pi" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Serra Gelada – Circular from Benidorm via Altea Port and Alfás del Pi&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a challenging loop through the natural park.

Q: What are some scenic points of interest I can visit while cycling?
A: Benidorm's routes are rich with scenic spots. You can cycle to viewpoints like The Cross, offering panoramic city and coastal views, or explore natural features such as the Sierra Helada Natural Park. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952029024/benidorm-the-cross-and-tossal-de-la-cala-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benidorm: The Cross and Tossal de la Cala Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a great option for combining these.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by waterfalls or reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. The region is home to beautiful natural water features. You can cycle towards the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/398248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Algar Springs Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, known for their clear lagoons. Additionally, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31809175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Castle – Guadalest Reservoir loop from l'Alfàs del Pi&lt;/a&gt; will take you past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/73676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Benidorm?
A: The touring cycling routes in Benidorm are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 280 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from coastal paths to challenging mountain climbs, and the stunning views encountered along the way.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, Benidorm offers 14 easy routes perfect for families or those new to touring cycling. Coastal promenades, such as sections of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31691050" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcón del Mediterráneo – Poniente Beach Promenade loop from Levante Beach&lt;/a&gt;, provide gentle, flat rides with beautiful sea views.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Benidorm?
A: Benidorm benefits from a mild Mediterranean climate year-round, making it an ideal destination for cycling almost any day. The pleasant temperatures and abundant sunshine, especially outside of the peak summer heat, create excellent conditions for exploring the routes.

Q: Are there historical sites or cultural attractions accessible by bike?
A: Yes, you can cycle to several historical and cultural points of interest. The historic village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/294803" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de Guadalest&lt;/a&gt;, with its castle and stunning views, is a popular destination accessible by bike, often involving some challenging climbs.

Q: Are there dedicated cycling lanes in Benidorm?
A: Yes, Benidorm has dedicated cycling lanes along main roads and beachfront areas. These lanes enhance the cycling experience, making it safer and more enjoyable for both locals and tourists to explore the city and its surroundings by bike.

Q: What are some challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes venturing into the Sierra de Aitana or Puig Campana offer demanding climbs and spectacular views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31809515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Highest Point of the Church – Illeta de l'Olla Beach loop from l'Alfàs del Pi&lt;/a&gt; is another difficult option with significant elevation gain.
tips: The altitude of Alto del Gobernador is about 435 meters. We will reach the top after 2,100 meters of route from the start of the Sierra Helada Natural Park.
One of the highest points in Parc Serra Gelada. Excellent viewpoint that offers stunning views of Altea and Calpe. Recommended, worth it!

A truly beautiful sight: Altea's small harbor lies at the transition between the promenade and the coast, blending harmoniously into the seascape. Not all areas are fenced off – some zones are freely accessible and invite you to take a short stroll. From the town center or directly from the promenade, a flat, pleasant route leads to the Club Náutico Altea. The route runs predominantly along quiet streets or wide sections of the promenade and is ideal for both leisurely bike rides and short daily commutes. You'll be accompanied almost constantly by a view of the sea, and the harbor itself is hard to miss: it stands prominently at the southern end of the beach promenade.
